_description: Phase 3 complete summary: COROT-01, COROT-02, COROT-03 results for Phase 4 loading
_units: SI throughout: m, kg, s, N, J, W, Pa, rad/s, dimensionless

COROT-01_angular_momentum.f_ss_note: Upper bound from smooth-cylinder approximation (vessel chain treated as continuous rotating inner cylinder). Discrete-vessel geometry correction reduces coupling by approximately factor of 2. Actual f_ss likely 0.3-0.5 * f_ss_upper_bound.
COROT-01_angular_momentum.P_corot_note: Nominal P_corot at omega_design. Range spans factor-of-2 uncertainty from Prandtl C_f ±50%.
COROT-01_angular_momentum.spin_up_note: tau_spinup ~ R^2/nu_T = 71 s; marginal quasi-steady; steady-state analysis is an approximation.
COROT-01_angular_momentum.SUMMARY_reconciliation: SUMMARY.md used ~1300 W. This derivation gives P_corot_nominal = 22194 W (factor 17.07x). Discrepancy from different C_f assumption and A_wall geometry. This derivation uses tau_w=0.5*rho_w*C_f*(omega*R)^2 consistently (Schlichting §21.2).

COROT-02_net_benefit.P_drag_full_note: Baseline drag power for 24 vessels at v_tan_design=3.34233 m/s with C_D=1.0, A_frontal=0.164 m².
COROT-02_net_benefit.P_drag_saved_at_fss_note: Cubic formula P_drag_saved = P_drag_full * [1-(1-f_stall)^3] at f=f_stall=0.294003.
COROT-02_net_benefit.P_corot_at_fss_note: P_corot at f_stall (much less than nominal 22194 W at design omega; omega_w = f_stall * omega_design = 0.268 rad/s).
COROT-02_net_benefit.P_net_at_fss_note: PITFALL-C3 compliant: P_corot always subtracted. P_net = P_drag_saved - P_corot.
COROT-02_net_benefit.P_net_range_note: [optimistic: P_corot×0.5, pessimistic: P_corot×2.0]. Both positive → net_positive verdict.
COROT-02_net_benefit.f_optimal_note: f_optimal = f_stall; P_net is stall-limited (P_corot remains small relative to P_drag_saved across entire achievable f range).
COROT-02_net_benefit.f_breakeven_note: No breakeven within [0, f_stall]; P_net > 0 throughout achievable domain.
COROT-02_net_benefit.COP_corot_at_fss_note: COP_corot at f_stall ≈ 0.603 (buoyancy-only COP). Foil contribution → 0 as lambda_eff → lambda_max at stall. COP_corot decreases from 2.057 at f=0 to 0.603 at f_stall.
COROT-02_net_benefit.phase3_verdict_explanation: P_net_lo=47.186 kW > 0 and P_net_hi=46.105 kW > 0 across full ±50% P_corot uncertainty range. Co-rotation saves ~47.5 kW of drag power while costing only ~0.72 kW in maintenance at maximum achievable f.
COROT-02_net_benefit.validation_summary: All 5 validation checks PASS: P_net(f=0)=0, cubic(0.3)=0.657, cubic(0.5)=0.875, COP(f=0)=2.057, monotonicity(stall-limited).

COROT-03_lift_preservation.v_rel_vertical_independence_proof: v_rel_v = v_vessel_v - v_water_v = v_loop - 0 = v_loop. Water co-rotation is horizontal only; v_water_v = 0 identically. v_rel_v = v_loop for all f in [0,1].
COROT-03_lift_preservation.lift_preservation_verdict: PRESERVED: v_rel_v = v_loop = 3.7137 m/s independent of f. Hydrofoil lift is nonzero for all f < f_stall = 0.2940.
COROT-03_lift_preservation.stall_implication: Stall at f_stall = 0.2940 is AoA-exceeding-limit event, not lift-going-to-zero.

phase4_inputs.co_rotation_f_ss_note: Use f_stall as the effective maximum achievable f_ss (f_ss_upper_bound=0.635 from smooth-cylinder model exceeds stall boundary). Actual f_ss in practice likely lower than f_stall due to discrete-vessel geometry.
phase4_inputs.P_corot_W_note: P_corot at achievable f_ss = f_stall = 0.294003. Much less than nominal 22194 W at design omega because omega_w = f_stall * omega_design = 0.268 rad/s.
phase4_inputs.P_corot_range_note: [P_corot×0.5, P_corot×2.0] at f_stall. All values negligible compared to P_drag_saved.
phase4_inputs.COP_partial_Phase2_note: Phase 2 COP at lambda=0.9 before co-rotation effect. COP_corot decreases at higher f (foil forces reduce near stall).
phase4_inputs.F_vert_note: From Phase 2: F_vert/F_b_avg=1.15 >> 0.20; Phase 4 coupled (v_loop, omega) solution mandatory; Phase 2 and Phase 3 COP values are upper bounds.
phase4_inputs.phase3_uncertainty_note: P_corot and f_ss have factor-of-2 uncertainty from smooth-cylinder Taylor-Couette approximation. Net verdict net_positive is robust: P_net >> P_corot across all uncertainty cases.
phase4_inputs.COP_corot_trend_note: COP_corot is monotonically decreasing in f (from 2.057 at f=0 to 0.603 at f_stall). Phase 4 must use COP_corot_at_fss (not COP_partial_Phase2) for system balance at the co-rotating operating point.

P_net_table.description: P_net(f) at key f values for Phase 4 reference. All values in W.
P_net_table.note: f=0.29 ≈ f_stall=0.294003 (last achievable operating point before hydrofoil stall). P_corot is negligible compared to P_drag_saved throughout.
